title: Giving directions to a taxi driver
description: Flag down a red taxi and direct the driver to your destination
context: Hong Kong taxis are colour-coded: red for urban areas, green for the New Territories, blue for Lantau. Red taxis are the most common. Drivers appreciate it if you know your destination and can give cross-street landmarks. Always have the Chinese characters for your destination ready.
culturalFact: The 'red taxi' is as iconic to Hong Kong as the black cab is to London. The city has around 18,000 licensed taxis and the red ones can cover all of Hong Kong Island and Kowloon — but crossing to the New Territories costs an extra tunnel toll.

lines.speaker: you
lines.jyutping: m4 goi1, heoi3 tung4 lo4 waan1.
lines.english: Excuse me, to Causeway Bay please.

lines.speaker: driver
lines.jyutping: heoi3 tung4 lo4 waan1 bin1 dou6?

lines.speaker: you
lines.cjk: 去時代廣場附近，謝斐道。
lines.jyutping: heoi3 si4 doi6 gwong2 coeng4 fu6 gan6, ze6 fei1 dou6.

lines.speaker: driver
lines.jyutping: hou2, haang4 seoi6 dou6 ding6 haang4 dei6 min6?
lines.english: OK, through the tunnel or on surface roads?

lines.speaker: you
lines.jyutping: haang4 dei6 min6 laa1, m4 gon2 si4 gaan3.
lines.english: Surface roads please, I'm not in a rush.

lines.speaker: driver
lines.jyutping: hou2, dou3 ga3 laa3, hai6 saam1 sap6 ng5 man1.

lines.speaker: you
lines.cjk: 唔該，呢係四十蚊，唔使找。
lines.jyutping: m4 goi1, ni1 hai6 sei3 sap6 man1, m4 sai2 zaau2.
lines.english: Here's forty dollars, keep the change.
